senses.gloss: a work of literature, music, or art

examples.text: وَحَكَى الْجَاحِظُ فِي بَعْضِ مُصَنَّفَاتِهِ أَنَّ بَهْرَام جُور، أَحَدَ مُلُوكِ الْفُرْسِ، لَمَّا مَلَكَ بَعْدَ أَبِيهِ يَزْدَجُرْدَ غَلَبَ الْعَدُوُّ عَلَى بَعْضِ أَطْرَافِ بِلَادِهِ، فَأَظْهَرَ ٱلِٱسْتِهَانَةَ بِأَمْرِ الْعَدُوِّ وَٱلِٱسْتِخْفَافَ بِهِ، حَتَّى قَوِيَ أَمْرُ الْعَدُوِّ، وَٱشْتَدَّتْ شَوْكَتُهُ، وَزَحَفَ نَحْوَهُ.
examples.english: Al-Jahiz recounts in one of his works that Bahram Gur, one of the kings of Persia, upon succeeding his father, Yazdagurd, had some of his frontier areas conquered by the enemy. He feigned inattention in the affair of the enemy and lightness towards it until the power of the enemy became strong and marched against him.

etymology.text:
Derived from the active participle of صَنَّفَ (ṣannafa).

Derived from the passive participle of صَنَّفَ (ṣannafa).

body.text: , which is wrong, for it is] like
Trees among which are two sorts, dry and fresh:
trees varying in colours and fruits.
